summary: Matched three previously stubbed Kirby special-move callbacks in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN.c: ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter, and ftKb_EatJump1_Anim. The PR body says all three were verified as 100% objdiff matches and ninja built cleanly; decomp.dev reported 3 new matches totaling +416 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Only changed file; replaced three /// # stubs with matching C implementations.

what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim. When animation frames are exhausted, it calls ftCommon_8007D5D4, changes motion state to ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi2 with flags 0xA, installs efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks, and clears fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x0.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter. It resets fp->cmd_vars[0], applies da->specials_aerial_vertical_momentum to fp->self_vel.y only if fp->fv.kb.x64 is false, then sets that flag true, enters ftKb_MS_SpecialAirS, calls ftAnim_8006EBA4, and sets accessory4_cb to fn_800F53AC.

Implemented ftKb_EatJump1_Anim. When animation frames end, it gets Kirby attrs, uses ftCommon_8007D5D4, changes to ftKb_MS_EatJump2 with flags 0x92, calls ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ftCo_800CB110 with da->specialn_jump_height.
Added explicit PAD_STACK(8) in ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter and PAD_STACK(24) in ftKb_EatJump1_Anim to preserve stack layout for matching.
decomp.dev reported ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter at +144 bytes, ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim at +136 bytes, and ftKb_EatJump1_Anim at +136 bytes, all moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Kept animation callbacks gated on !ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining before performing state transitions, matching the callback style shown in the diff and the PR description.
Used existing fighter helper flow instead of bespoke logic: GET_FIGHTER, ftCommon_8007D5D4, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ftCo_800CB110.
Pulled movement constants from ftKb_DatAttrs rather than hard-coding gameplay tuning values: specials_aerial_vertical_momentum and specialn_jump_height.
Preserved side-effect ordering around motion changes, callbacks, and flags, which is likely important for both runtime behavior and instruction matching.
Used PAD_STACK only where needed by the matched functions, a targeted matching tactic that avoided broader structural churn.
decomp_lessons: Kirby special-move callbacks in ftKb_SpecialN.c often combine motion-state transitions with small fighter-field side effects; future matches should inspect both Fighter_ChangeMotionState parameters and adjacent callback/flag assignments.
For animation-ending transitions, the pattern !ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) followed by ftCommon_8007D5D4 and Fighter_ChangeMotionState is reusable.
Aerial-entry functions may have one-time momentum behavior guarded by a fighter-var flag; here fp->fv.kb.x64 prevents repeatedly applying specials_aerial_vertical_momentum.
Do not rename uncertain fighter fields prematurely. This patch kept placeholder-style fields such as fp->fv.kb.x64 and fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x0 while still producing matches.
Small functions can require explicit PAD_STACK padding to satisfy objdiff; stack padding is a valid localized tactic when the surrounding logic is otherwise understood.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix.
Callback suffixes identify role: _Anim for animation callbacks and _Enter for state entry functions.
Kirby motion-state enum names use ftKb_MS_* such as ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi2, ftKb_MS_SpecialAirS, and ftKb_MS_EatJump2.
Common local variable names in this file are Fighter* fp for the fighter object and ftKb_DatAttrs* da for Kirby data attributes.
Unidentified callbacks and fields retain project placeholder naming, e.g. fn_800F53AC, fp->fv.kb.x64, and fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK(8) was used in ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter and PAD_STACK(24) in ftKb_EatJump1_Anim, showing stack-shape control was required for these matches.
Preserved exact motion-state flag immediates from the match: 0xA for ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi2, 0 for ftKb_MS_SpecialAirS, and 0x92 for ftKb_MS_EatJump2.
Used explicit float literals in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls: 0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f.
Placed GET_FIGHTER and dat_attrs access in the same narrow scopes shown by the matched implementation, including inside the EatJump1 frame-ended branch.
Verification evidence came from objdiff and decomp.dev: all three functions reached 100.00%, and ninja built cleanly according to the PR body.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback confirmed 3 new matches and +416 matched bytes for GALE01.
follow_up_queries: Search for other Kirby aerial special entry functions using fp->fv.kb.x64 to clarify that field name.
Compare ftKb_SpecialS_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter for shared Hammer accessory callback behavior around fn_800F53AC.
Audit uses of ftKb_MS_EatJump2 and ftCo_800CB110 to improve naming around Kirby swallowed-opponent jump behavior.
Look for other matches requiring PAD_STACK in small fighter callbacks to reuse stack-padding patterns.
